differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Nephrotic syndrome (e.g., Membranous Nephropathy) with secondary hypercoagulability
Infectious pneumonia with secondary reactive thrombotic complications
next_steps: Perform comprehensive serologic workup including ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-Smith, complement levels (C3, C4), and antiphospholipid antibodies (lupus anticoagulant, anticardiolipin, anti-beta2-glycoprotein I)
Initiate therapeutic anticoagulation (e.g., heparin infusion) due to documented renal vein thrombosis and suspected pulmonary embolism
Perform kidney biopsy to differentiate between lupus nephritis, membranous nephropathy, and other glomerular pathologies
Assess for complement consumption and hemolytic markers (haptoglobin, LDH, peripheral blood smear) to rule out TMA
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotics pending final blood and sputum cultures
Monitor respiratory status closely and consider diagnostic thoracentesis if pleural effusions persist or worsen
Consult nephrology and rheumatology for specialized management of suspected autoimmune disease

differential_diagnoses: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus with lupus nephritis and secondary antiphospholipid syndrome
Membranous nephropathy complicated by hypercoagulable state and pulmonary infection
Polyarteritis nodosa with renal and pulmonary involvement
Infected renal vein thrombosis with septic pulmonary emboli
next_steps: Initiate full-dose therapeutic anticoagulation despite hematuria, given the high risk of catastrophic thrombosis extension
Perform comprehensive autoimmune and serological workup: ANA, anti-dsDNA, anti-Smith, C3, C4, and full antiphospholipid antibody panel
Perform transthoracic echocardiography to assess for right heart strain, valvular vegetation, or intracardiac thrombus
Perform diagnostic thoracentesis to differentiate between inflammatory/nephrotic effusion and septic/parapneumonic process
Consult nephrology and rheumatology for urgent management and planning of kidney biopsy once anticoagulation/hemodynamics allow
Obtain serial blood and sputum cultures to guide targeted antimicrobial therapy
Perform assessment of hemolytic markers (haptoglobin, LDH, peripheral blood smear) to rule out concurrent thrombotic microangiopathy
